description: Replace --dangerously-skip-permissions with scoped --allowedTools + --permission-mode dontAsk, add --enable-auto-mode support (disabled by default), and add deprecation hint for auto mode.

criteria: Rust: No warnings from cargo check or cargo clippy -- -D warnings
Rust: All tests pass with cargo test
No breaking changes to existing public APIs

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works
3. CORRECTNESS - Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Each task delivers working code + tests together
Test boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: PermissionMode enum exists with Dangerous, Scoped { allowed_tools: Option<String> }, Auto variants
Positive: CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS constant contains Read,Edit,Write,Bash(cargo *),Bash(git *),Bash(task-mgr *),Bash(mkdir *),Bash(ls *),Bash(wc *),Bash(head *),Bash(tail *),Bash(cat *),Glob,Grep
Positive: permission_mode_from_env() returns Scoped with CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS when no env vars set
Unit test: with LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE=dangerous, permission_mode_from_env() returns Dangerous
Unit test: with LOOP_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=true, permission_mode_from_env() returns Auto
Unit test: with LOOP_ALLOWED_TOOLS=Read,Bash, permission_mode_from_env() returns Scoped { allowed_tools: Some('Read,Bash') }
Unit test: with both LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE=dangerous AND LOOP_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=true, returns Dangerous (dangerous wins)
Unit test: with LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE=unknown, falls through to Scoped default

Known-bad: if auto mode is checked before dangerous mode, then setting both LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE=dangerous and LOOP_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=true would incorrectly return Auto instead of Dangerous. Test must set both and verify Dangerous wins.
Negative: PermissionMode is NOT added as a field on LoopConfig
Positive: parse_bool_value is made pub(crate) so engine.rs can use it for the deprecation hint
userStories.description:
Add the PermissionMode enum to src/loop_engine/config.rs with three variants: Dangerous, Scoped { allowed_tools: Option<String> }, and Auto. Add CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS constant and permission_mode_from_env() function.

DO: Follow existing config.rs patterns — use parse_env_bool() for LOOP_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE, std::env::var for LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE and LOOP_ALLOWED_TOOLS.
DO: Derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq on the enum (matches existing config enums).
DO: Make parse_bool_value public (currently private, needed by engine.rs for the deprecation hint).

DO NOT: Add PermissionMode to LoopConfig struct — it's resolved per-call-site, not a global config field.
DO NOT: Add any CLI flags yet — this is env-var-only configuration.

Edge cases:
- LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE=dangerous takes precedence over LOOP_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=true (explicit dangerous overrides auto)
- LOOP_ALLOWED_TOOLS env var overrides the default CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS constant
- Unrecognized LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE values fall through to the default (Scoped)

Known-bad to guard against:
- Checking LOOP_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE before LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE would let auto mode be accidentally overridden by the default Scoped path. The check order must be: dangerous first, then auto, then scoped default.
userStories.notes: Reuse existing parse_env_bool() at config.rs:144. Follow the same env var naming convention (LOOP_* prefix). The CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS constant value comes from analysis of scripts/prompt.md — the autonomous agent needs these tools for cargo, git, task-mgr CLI, and file operations. NOTE: env var tests that call std::env::set_var must use the ENV_MUTEX pattern from claude.rs tests to avoid parallel test interference, OR use pure-function tests on parse_bool_value/parse_value instead.
userStories.title: Add PermissionMode enum and env var parsing to config.rs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: spawn_claude signature has permission_mode: &PermissionMode as last parameter
Positive: args Vec is now Vec<String> instead of Vec<&str>
Positive: Dangerous mode includes --dangerously-skip-permissions in args
Positive: Scoped mode includes --permission-mode dontAsk in args
Positive: Scoped with Some(tools) includes --allowedTools <tools> after --permission-mode dontAsk
Positive: Auto mode includes --enable-auto-mode in args
Negative: Scoped with None does NOT include --allowedTools in args
Negative: Scoped mode does NOT include --dangerously-skip-permissions
Negative: Auto mode does NOT include --dangerously-skip-permissions
Positive: Existing arg ordering preserved: base flags → permission flags → [--model] → -p <prompt>
Positive: Code compiles (cargo check) — callers will temporarily break until FEAT-003, but the function itself compiles
Failure mode: if Command::args() fails with Vec<String>, fall back to .args(args.iter().map(|s| s.as_str())) — but this should not happen since args() accepts IntoIterator<Item: AsRef<OsStr>>
userStories.description:
Change the spawn_claude function signature to accept permission_mode: &PermissionMode as the last parameter. Replace the hardcoded --dangerously-skip-permissions with permission-mode-aware arg construction. Change args from Vec<&str> to Vec<String> to support owned tool list strings.

DO: Match on permission_mode to emit different flags:
  - Dangerous → --dangerously-skip-permissions
  - Scoped { allowed_tools: Some(t) } → --permission-mode dontAsk --allowedTools <t>
  - Scoped { allowed_tools: None } → --permission-mode dontAsk (no --allowedTools)
  - Auto → --enable-auto-mode
DO: Keep the existing arg ordering: mode flags → [--model <m>] → -p <prompt>
DO: Convert model and prompt args to String too for consistency.

DO NOT: Change the function's other behavior (stdout tee, watchdog, process group, etc.)
DO NOT: Update callers in this task — callers are updated in FEAT-003.

Edge cases:
- Scoped with None allowed_tools must NOT emit --allowedTools flag at all
- The --permission-mode and dontAsk are two separate args (not --permission-mode=dontAsk)

Known-bad to guard against:
- Passing --allowedTools with an empty string is different from omitting it entirely. Scoped { allowed_tools: None } must omit the flag, not pass --allowedTools ''.
userStories.notes: The function is at src/loop_engine/claude.rs:67-209. Current args construction is lines 76-98. Command::args() at line 101 accepts any IntoIterator<Item: AsRef<OsStr>>, so Vec<String> works directly. Update the doc comments (lines 1-9, 44-66) to reflect the new behavior. Import PermissionMode from config at the top of the file.
userStories.title: Modify spawn_claude to accept PermissionMode and build args accordingly

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: IterationParams has new field permission_mode: &'a PermissionMode
Positive: run_loop() calls config::permission_mode_from_env() once and stores the result
Positive: engine.rs spawn_claude call passes params.permission_mode
Positive: learnings/ingestion/mod.rs passes &PermissionMode::Scoped { allowed_tools: None }
Positive: curate/enrich.rs passes &PermissionMode::Scoped { allowed_tools: None }
Positive: curate/mod.rs passes &PermissionMode::Scoped { allowed_tools: None }
Positive: watchdog.rs tests pass &PermissionMode::Dangerous
Positive: cargo check passes with zero errors across all modules
Negative: main loop caller does NOT pass Scoped { allowed_tools: None } — it must include the coding tools

Known-bad: passing None tools to the engine.rs caller would compile fine but break all iterations at runtime. Verify engine.rs call uses the permission_mode from env (which defaults to Scoped with CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS).
userStories.description:
Update the 4 production call sites and 2 test call sites that invoke spawn_claude to pass the new permission_mode parameter.

DO: Use these permission modes per call site:
  1. engine.rs:459 (main loop) → pass permission_mode from IterationParams (resolved from env)
  2. learnings/ingestion/mod.rs:77 → Scoped { allowed_tools: None } (text-only analysis)
  3. curate/enrich.rs:234 → Scoped { allowed_tools: None } (text-only analysis)
  4. curate/mod.rs:613 → Scoped { allowed_tools: None } (text-only analysis)
  5. watchdog.rs test (line 308) → Dangerous (test with mock binary)
  6. watchdog.rs test (line 362) → Dangerous (test with mock binary)

DO: Add permission_mode: &'a PermissionMode field to IterationParams struct (engine.rs:~94).
DO: Resolve permission_mode once in run_loop() near Step 15 (~line 1176) using config::permission_mode_from_env(), store it, and pass to IterationParams.

DO NOT: Add the deprecation warning in this task — that's FEAT-004.

Edge cases:
- The learning/curate callers are text-only (no tool use) — passing Scoped with None ensures they get --permission-mode dontAsk without any --allowedTools, which means Claude operates in pure text-in/text-out mode.

Known-bad to guard against:
- If the main loop caller passes Scoped { allowed_tools: None } instead of Scoped { allowed_tools: Some(CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS) }, Claude won't have access to Read/Edit/Bash and every iteration will fail silently.
userStories.notes: Import paths needed: use crate::loop_engine::config::PermissionMode in curate/enrich.rs, curate/mod.rs, learnings/ingestion/mod.rs. For watchdog.rs tests, import PermissionMode from the parent module. The IterationParams struct is at engine.rs:~94-126. The run_loop() function starts at engine.rs:670.
userStories.title: Update all spawn_claude callers with appropriate PermissionMode

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: When LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=true and mode is Scoped, hint message prints to stderr
Positive: Hint includes text about --enable-auto-mode and LOOP_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=true
Positive: Hint uses yellow ANSI color code for [hint] prefix
Negative: When LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE is unset, no hint prints
Negative: When LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=false, no hint prints
Negative: When mode is Auto, no hint prints even with LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=true
Positive: Hint is placed between Step 15 (banner) and Step 16 (usage params) in run_loop()
userStories.description:
Add a one-time hint message that prints at loop start when auto mode is available but not enabled. The hint fires when LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=true is set AND the resolved permission mode is NOT Auto.

DO: Insert the check after Step 15 (session banner, ~line 1176) and before Step 16 (usage params).
DO: Use eprintln! with yellow ANSI color for the [hint] prefix, matching existing warning patterns.
DO: Make the hint actionable — tell the user exactly what env var to set.
DO: Use config::parse_bool_value (now pub(crate) from FEAT-001) to parse the env var.

DO NOT: Print the hint if the user already has auto mode enabled.
DO NOT: Use any external crate for colored output — raw ANSI codes are fine (eprintln! goes to stderr).

Edge cases:
- LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE not set → no hint (silent)
- LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=false → no hint
- LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=true + PermissionMode::Auto → no hint (already enabled)
- LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=true + PermissionMode::Dangerous → show hint (they should switch)
- LOOP_AUTO_MODE_AVAILABLE=true + PermissionMode::Scoped → show hint
userStories.notes: The display::print_session_banner call is at engine.rs:1169-1175. Insert the hint check at ~line 1176. Use config::parse_bool_value(&val) for parsing. The permission_mode variable is already resolved from FEAT-003.
userStories.title: Add auto mode deprecation hint in engine.rs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: spawn_claude_echo accepts permission_mode: &PermissionMode parameter
Positive: All 6 existing tests pass with updated assertions
Unit test: test_permission_mode_dangerous — output contains --dangerously-skip-permissions, does NOT contain --permission-mode
Unit test: test_permission_mode_scoped_with_tools — output contains --permission-mode dontAsk --allowedTools <tools>, does NOT contain --dangerously-skip-permissions
Unit test: test_permission_mode_scoped_no_tools — output contains --permission-mode dontAsk, does NOT contain --allowedTools, does NOT contain --dangerously-skip-permissions
Unit test: test_permission_mode_auto — output contains --enable-auto-mode, does NOT contain --dangerously-skip-permissions, does NOT contain --permission-mode
Positive: Exact-string test updated to new default scoped args format
Positive: Ordering tests updated to verify permission flags come after --no-session-persistence and before [--model] -p
Positive: All tests in cargo test --lib loop_engine::claude pass
Known-bad: if a test still asserts --dangerously-skip-permissions for the default mode, it would pass with the old code but fail with the new code — all such assertions must be updated
userStories.description:
Update all 6 existing tests in claude.rs that assert on --dangerously-skip-permissions, and add new tests covering all PermissionMode variants.

DO: Update spawn_claude_echo helper to accept permission_mode parameter.
DO: Update existing tests to pass a PermissionMode (most should use Scoped with tools since that's the new default).
DO: Update assertions from checking --dangerously-skip-permissions to checking --permission-mode dontAsk.
DO: Update the exact-string test (test_spawn_claude_none_model_identical_to_pre_phase2) to match new arg format.
DO: Add new tests for each PermissionMode variant.

DO NOT: Change test logic for non-permission-related behavior (model handling, timeout, stream-json, etc.).

Existing tests to update:
1. test_spawn_model_none_no_model_flag (line 743)
2. test_spawn_model_some_preserves_required_flags (line 835)
3. test_spawn_claude_model_does_not_interfere_with_flags (line 924)
4. test_spawn_claude_none_model_identical_to_pre_phase2 (line 987)
5. test_stream_json_false_uses_print_flag (line 1052)
6. test_stream_json_true_with_model_and_timeout_arg_ordering (line 1656)

New tests to add:
- test_permission_mode_dangerous_uses_skip_flag
- test_permission_mode_scoped_with_tools
- test_permission_mode_scoped_no_tools
- test_permission_mode_auto

Known-bad to guard against:
- The exact-string test (pre_phase2) is the strictest validator. If the new arg format is wrong, this test catches it. Update its expected string carefully.
userStories.notes: The spawn_claude_echo helper is at claude.rs:457-468. The ENV_MUTEX pattern is at claude.rs:454. Tests use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo to capture args. For the exact-string test, the new expected output for Scoped mode with stream_json=false would be: '--print --no-session-persistence --permission-mode dontAsk --allowedTools <tools> -p my prompt text'. For tests that just need to compile and aren't testing permission modes specifically, use &PermissionMode::Scoped { allowed_tools: Some(CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS.to_string()) } as the default.
userStories.title: Update existing spawn_claude tests + add new permission mode tests

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: claude.rs module doc (line 1-9) no longer references --dangerously-skip-permissions as the only mode
Positive: spawn_claude doc comment documents the permission_mode parameter and its three variants
Positive: No stale references to --dangerously-skip-permissions as 'always present' in doc comments
Positive: cargo doc generates clean documentation
userStories.description:
Update all doc comments that reference --dangerously-skip-permissions to reflect the new permission model.

DO: Update the module-level doc comment at claude.rs:1-9 to describe the three permission modes.
DO: Update the spawn_claude function doc comment (claude.rs:44-66) to document the permission_mode parameter.
DO: Update any references in engine.rs comments that mention the old flag.

DO NOT: Add doc comments to functions you didn't change.
DO NOT: Change any logic — this is documentation only.
userStories.notes: Search for all occurrences of 'dangerously-skip-permissions' in doc comments (not in match arms or test assertions). Key locations: claude.rs:3, claude.rs:46.
userStories.title: Update doc comments and module documentation

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: No duplicated multi-line patterns appearing 3+ times without extraction
Positive: spawn_claude function remains readable (linear orchestration is OK per learning 328)
Positive: Test helpers are ergonomic — common cases don't require boilerplate
Positive: cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes with zero warnings
userStories.description:
Review all changes for opportunities to improve code quality.

DO: Check for duplicated PermissionMode::Scoped { allowed_tools: None } construction across callers — if 3+ sites use the same pattern, consider a helper constant or constructor.
DO: Check function lengths — spawn_claude was already 142 lines; verify the permission match didn't push it over a readability threshold.
DO: Check test helper ergonomics — if most tests pass the same PermissionMode, consider a default wrapper.

DO NOT: Over-abstract — if the duplication is just 2 lines, a helper isn't worth it.
userStories.notes: Learning 328: 'Long linear orchestration functions are acceptable if splitting scatters flow.' Don't split spawn_claude just for line count — it's linear orchestration. But DO extract if the permission match block could be a standalone function for testing.
userStories.title: Review for DRY, testability, and code quality

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All new code reachable from production entry points
Positive: All errors propagated with context (map_err, not unwrap)
Positive: cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean
Positive: permission_mode flows correctly: env → run_loop → IterationParams → spawn_claude
Positive: CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS matches what prompt.md instructs the agent to use
Positive: No stale --dangerously-skip-permissions references in production code or doc comments
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths
Negative: No dead code warnings for new code
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON with priority 50-98
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring. Run full test suite.

DO: Check every new function for error handling, check callers still work, verify no dead code.
DO: Verify the CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS constant covers all tools the prompt.md instructs Claude to use.
DO: Verify the env var precedence chain works correctly (dangerous > auto > scoped).
DO: Check that text-only callers (learning extraction, curate) truly don't need any tools.
DO: Verify no stale references to --dangerously-skip-permissions in non-test code.

DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — verify permission_mode flows from run_loop → IterationParams → spawn_claude.
DO NOT: Skip checking that the escape hatch (LOOP_PERMISSION_MODE=dangerous) actually works end-to-end.
userStories.notes: If issues found: add FIX-xxx tasks to JSON, commit JSON changes. Loop picks them up automatically. If no issues: mark passes:true with note 'Clean review'.
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
